analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition

operators: aten::addmm (1 node, bias epilogue feeding the same GEMMs)

bottleneck.description: Every matmul kernel ('Kernel2', the anonymized cuBLAS GEMM) runs entirely on the FP32 FFMA/SIMT path with Tensor Cores completely idle. cuBLAS routes FP32 dense matmul away from the Tensor Core MMA pipes, so on this Blackwell GB202 part the entire compute budget (~98% of de-duplicated attributed time, all 8 distinct GEMMs) executes at SIMT throughput. The SIMT GEMM also runs at very high register pressure (200-210 registers/thread), which caps achieved occupancy at 8-17% and leaves the SMs latency/occupancy starved rather than compute saturated.

transformation.description: Promote the matmul operands from FP32 to BF16 so cuBLAS dispatches to the Blackwell Tensor Core MMA path instead of the FFMA/SIMT path. At the Aten IR level, insert aten._to_copy (dtype=torch.bfloat16) on the activation and weight inputs of each aten::mm.default / aten::addmm.default node, leaving the accumulation in FP32 (cuBLAS BF16-in/FP32-acc). The matmul result is cast back to FP32 with a single aten._to_copy so the downstream activation epilogue and final output are unchanged. If BF16 numerics are unacceptable, the lower-risk alternative is to keep FP32 storage and set torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True (and torch.backends.cudnn.allow_tf32 = True), which routes FP32 matmul through the TF32 Tensor Core path with no graph surgery and ~1e-3 relative error -- this is a global flag, not an FX pass, but achieves the same Tensor-Core engagement at lower accuracy cost than full BF16.
transformation.location: Inductor post_grad_custom_pre_pass (Aten IR), applied before lowering to the cuBLAS/Triton backend. Per torch 2.11 on this system, use post_grad_custom_pre_pass -- the aot_autograd fw_compiler injection point is broken on 2.11.
fx_steps: # Operate on the post-grad Aten IR graph module gm.graph
if node.op != 'call_function' or node.target not in MM_TARGETS:
base = 1 if node.target is torch.ops.aten.addmm.default else 0
# cast the matmul result back to fp32 for the downstream activation epilogue
back.args = (node,)  # restore self-reference broken by replace_all_uses_with
transformation.code_hint: Simplest validated path: at model build time set torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True (TF32 Tensor Core path, no FX surgery). For full BF16, register the pass above via torch._inductor.config.post_grad_custom_pre_pass and clear the Inductor cache before re-profiling (stale artifacts cause false results).

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Weight/activation matmul reads halved (FP32->BF16 operands); the 2048x2048 GEMMs each read ~19 MB of operands, halved to ~9.5 MB. Dominant gain is compute (Tensor Core engagement), not traffic.

optimizations.notes: Mechanism is unambiguous: tensor_core_active_pct is exactly 0.0 on all 8 GEMMs while DRAM throughput is only 3-9%, so these kernels are neither memory-bound nor using Tensor Cores. The reduction estimate is conservative and relative only -- duration_ns is 2-5x inflated by ncu counter collection and must not be read as absolute latency. The 2048x512 / 512x2048 GEMMs (op_id 3,9,13,19) benefit most because their 8.3% occupancy and 200 regs/thread reflect a SIMT GEMM that BF16 MMA tiling avoids entirely.

bottleneck.description: Each Linear layer emits TWO kernels: the cuBLAS GEMM ('Kernel2') plus a separate Triton elementwise kernel ('triton_poi_fused_addmm_<act>') that applies the bias add and the activation. Because cuBLAS owns the matmul, Inductor cannot fuse the bias+activation into the GEMM epilogue, so the full [256,2048] GEMM output is written to DRAM by the GEMM kernel and re-read by the activation kernel, then written again. The activation kernels are memory-bound (DRAM throughput 26-34% for relu/gelu/silu) and the tanh epilogue reads an anomalous ~532 MB, indicating a poor special-function memory access pattern. These are 8 extra launches whose only purpose is a DRAM round-trip of the activation tensor.

transformation.description: Let Inductor own the matmul so the bias add and activation fuse into the GEMM epilogue, eliminating the separate Triton activation kernels and the full-tensor DRAM round-trip. Enable max-autotune so Inductor emits a Triton matmul template with epilogue fusion of the aten::add (bias) and the aten::relu/gelu/silu/tanh node directly onto the matmul output tile while it is still resident in registers/shared memory. At the Aten IR level the targets are the aten::addmm.default / aten::mm.default nodes followed by aten::relu.default, aten::gelu.default, aten::silu.default, aten::tanh.default; the policy marks these chains for epilogue fusion rather than allowing the matmul to be lowered to the opaque cuBLAS extern call. Most effective once OPT-1 has set the operands to BF16, because the Tensor Core MMA template keeps the output tile on-chip and the epilogue runs on the accumulator before write-back.
transformation.location: Inductor configuration + lowering policy: set torch._inductor.config.max_autotune_gemm = True and torch._inductor.config.epilogue_fusion = True so the matmul+bias+activation chain is lowered to a single fused Triton template kernel.
fx_steps: ind.max_autotune_gemm = True       # enable Triton GEMM templates that can fuse epilogues
ind.epilogue_fusion = True         # fuse aten::add(bias)+activation onto the GEMM output tile
ind.max_autotune_gemm_backends = 'TRITON'  # force the Triton template path (cuBLAS extern cannot fuse epilogue)
# No node insertion needed: the fusion is enabled by lowering policy. Verify in the
# Inductor debug dir that triton_poi_fused_addmm_<act> kernels are gone and a single
# triton_tem_fused_addmm_<act> (templated GEMM+epilogue) kernel appears per layer.
transformation.code_hint: Combine with OPT-1 (BF16) for best result. After changing Inductor config, clear the Inductor cache (stale compiled artifacts cause false negatives) and re-profile. Confirm tensor_core_active_pct > 0 on the new templated kernels.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Eliminates one full DRAM write + read of each [256,2048] activation tensor (~2 MB/layer in FP32, ~1 MB in BF16) per layer, plus the anomalous ~532 MB tanh-epilogue read. Net: ~8 fewer kernel launches and the activation tensors never leave on-chip memory between matmul and activation.

optimizations.notes: Pure-kernel-time saving is small (1.7% of attributed time) because the activation kernels are already tiny; the real value is removing 8 launches and the DRAM round-trip, which matters more in the real (un-inflated) timeline than the ncu-inflated profile suggests. Confidence is medium: epilogue fusion of activations onto a Triton GEMM template is well supported, but whether max-autotune selects the Triton template over cuBLAS for these specific shapes is autotuner-dependent and must be verified in the Inductor debug output. Capped at medium because external pattern search (exa-search/context7) was unavailable in this environment.

global_notes: Knowledge files knowledge/hardware-limits.md and knowledge/fx-patterns.md were not present in this checkout, and no MCP tools (context7, exa-search, memory, sequential-thinking) were available. Per fallback policy, analysis proceeded from built-in Blackwell/FX knowledge and novel transforms were capped at medium confidence. SM count for RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition (GB202) taken as ~188; Waves/SM was not load-bearing for any proposal since all GEMMs are Tensor-Core-idle rather than wave-starved.

Blackwell architecture: warp_cycles_per_instruction is removed and is correctly null in the profile; latency-bound assessment used eligible_cycles_pct (<20% on the 2048x512 / 512x2048 GEMMs indicates occupancy/latency starvation driven by the FP32 SIMT register pressure).

fused_kernel_double_count flag: the profile contains overlapping aggregate operator buckets ('aten::mm_0' with 7 kernels / 743327 ns and 'layer::unique::prologue' with 17 kernels / 600894 ns) that re-include the same Kernel2 GEMMs and triton activation kernels also counted under the per-op aten::mm op_id=N entries and aten::addmm. To avoid double counting, the time budget was computed over the de-duplicated decomposition (8 distinct aten::mm GEMMs + 1 aten::addmm activation bucket = 890878 ns total); the two aggregate buckets were excluded from the budget.

dynamic_shapes flag: kernel_name 'Kernel2' shows >50% coefficient of variation in duration_ns (50.5k to 137k ns), but this is shape heterogeneity -- four distinct GEMM shapes (512x2048, 2048x2048, 2048x512) share a single anonymized cuBLAS kernel name -- not genuine dynamic-shape recompilation. Input is static [256,512].

warm_up_inflation flag: capture_metadata does not record measure_iters, so the multiple-of check cannot be evaluated precisely; the repeated GEMM/activation kernels (each distinct shape appears ~2x) and the dedicated prologue aggregate bucket are consistent with warm-up/dedup capture overlapping the measured iterations. Treat absolute kernel_count per op as approximate.
0 operators below the 1% threshold were omitted from the de-duplicated budget; all 9 retained operators are >= 1%.

All duration_ns figures are inflated 2-5x by ncu counter collection and are used only for relative comparison within this profile, never as absolute latency. Estimated impacts are relative reductions.

Dependency DAG: OPT-1 (dtype promotion to BF16) is prerequisite_for OPT-2 (epilogue fusion). OPT-2's epilogue fusion is most effective once operands are BF16 because the Tensor Core MMA template retains the output tile on-chip for the activation. No cycles. OPT-1 also unblocks the general rule that any register_buffer-allocating pass (none proposed here) must follow dtype promotion.
